headline: Melaka River Cleanup Removes 48 Tonnes of Waste Monthly
context: Melaka's river management authority removes an average of 48 tonnes of solid waste from Sungai Melaka monthly in 2025, largely unchanged from 2018 despite RM120M in upstream interventions.

cards.big: Melaka spent RM120M on upstream waste reduction since 2018.
cards.sub: Monthly waste pulled from the river remains unchanged at 48 tonnes. The spending had no measurable impact.

cards.big: 65% of river waste is single-use food packaging.
cards.sub: No mandatory packaging reduction policy exists at state or federal level.

cards.big: Sungai Melaka is Melaka's primary UNESCO World Heritage attraction.
cards.sub: Tourist complaints about river condition rose 340% on review platforms between 2020 and 2024.

cards.big: 16 drain outfalls discharge untreated stormwater carrying waste into the river.
cards.sub: Gross pollutant traps cost RM80,000 each. Installing 16 would cost RM1.28M — 1% of money already spent.

cards.big: RM120M spent upstream, 48 tonnes per month downstream. If the intervention worked, the number would change.
River cleanup programmes that do not change waste generation rates are treating symptoms permanently. Melaka needs source reduction — bans, deposits, producer responsibility — not endless collection.
